commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a `claude-cli-interactive` Anthropic CLI backend that runs Claude through a Bun TLS MITM wrapper to surface `thinking_delta` data, and also adds CI/script automation to refresh Claude CLI model allowlists.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence failure path: selecting `claude-cli-interactive` uses a provider id and synthetic output shape that the current parser and reasoning bridge do not consume; I did not run the live backend because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Maintainer/security/product review is needed before repair automation because the draft branch introduces local TLS interception and unrelated write-capable CI, and the current proof does not exercise the OpenClaw integration path.

target.title: feat(anthropic): claude-cli-interactive backend — stream reasoning via local TLS proxy (experimental)
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia
